howItWorks.intro: This curtain fabric calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Width: Window width × Fullness factor ÷ Panels + 6" (hems)
Standard fullness: 2× window width for nice draping
Add extra for pattern matching on prints
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much fabric do I need for curtains?
faq.answer: For standard 60" window with 2× fullness and 2 panels: about 5-6 yards of 54" fabric. Formula: multiply window width by fullness factor, divide by panels, add 6" for hems. Length = window height + 12" for hems.

faq.question: What is curtain fullness?
faq.answer: Fullness is how much wider the fabric is than the window. 1.5× = minimal gathering (modern/casual). 2× = standard (most curtains). 2.5× = luxurious full draping. Sheer curtains often use 3× for a billowy look.

faq.question: How long should curtains be?
faq.answer: Standard: just touching the floor or 1/2" above. Puddle: 2-3" extra on the floor (romantic look). Sill length: ending at the window sill. Apron length: 4-6" below the sill. Floor-length curtains are most common and look best.

faq.question: Is the Curtain Fabric Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Curtain Fabric Calculator?
sections.body: The Curtain Fabric Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your window width, window height, fullness, you get instant results including cut width per panel, cut length per panel, total fabric needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cut width per panel right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your window width and need to find the right cut width per panel. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Window Width (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your window width — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: window height, fullness, number of panels. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your cut width per panel, cut length per panel, total fabric needed. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your window width before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
